Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala



Welcome

Greetings! Thank you for visiting our website. Here you are able  to learn more about Jesus Christ, our church's purpose, and our ministry opportunities. For over 131 years, New Light has been known for, Exalting the Savior, Equipping the Saints, and Evangelizing the Sinners. We extend a personal invitation to you to worship with us at one of our two morning worship opportunities, and we look forward to seeing you at New Light !!!

	

Worship Service Opportunities- Join us for worship EVERY Sunday at 7:30am or 10:45am and Mid-Week Worship, EVERY Wednesday at 7:00pm. Services are avaliable both for In-person and Online. 

PRAYER LINE- Join us every Thursday at 8:00pm for prayer. Call (712)-775-7031/ Access Code: 769-681-374#





	


 	

Home About Us Our Leadership Media Contact Us Giving Summer Adventure 50th Anniversary Gala

Greensboro, NC 27401| Phone~336.273.5579| Email~ office@nlmbc.com

new_to_nlmbc.title: New Light Missionary Baptist Church - I Am New to NLMBC

sections.text:

Media

Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala
I Am New to NLMBC


Visiting a church can sometimes be a little overwhelming. If this is your first time as a guest, you may have lots of questions such as what time is service, is there praise & worship, where can I park, are there classes for children, etc. This page offers you a starting point with information for some of the first areas you may need assistance in! Should you have specific inquiries, please contact the church office at 336.273.5579

Service Schedules

Come join the NLMBC family at worship this week! Please join us for any of these opportunities to learn and grow in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ!

Christian Education Classes and Spiritual Enrichment

Our Christian Education ministry offers classes throughout the church calendar year that are biblical and basic; real and relevant in meeting the needs of the congregants to mature in their relationship with God, with others, and to become better followers of Christ.  We welcome and assist all to have an understanding of who they are, as the children of God, through the transforming power of God’s presence in their lives and to discover ways and means to respond to God’s unconditional love as disciples making disciples.

history.title: New Light Missionary Baptist Church - Our History

sections.text:
Home	


Email Login	


Contact us	



		
Home
About Us
Our Leadership
Media
Contact Us
Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala

History

N
 

ew Light Missionary Baptist Church is recognized as a pillar of strength in the Greater Greensboro Community. Its history dates back to the year 1891, and is a priceless heritage passed on to us by our ancestors through their love for Jesus Christ. Mr. and Mrs. Aaron Barnett donated land to build the new church under the leadership of Reverend Neil Brown. Soon after the death of Reverend Neil Brown, Reverend J. B. Ellis was called to pastoral duties. He served the church fifty-three years, and under his leadership, New Light Missionary Baptist Church on McConnell Road was erected.

Following the death of Reverend J. B. Ellis in 1951, Reverend S. G. Griffies was called to pastoral duties. During his tenure of service many architectural improvements were made to the edifice. A building fund drive was established which proved instrumental in purchasing the property on Willow Road. Reverend Albert Brown, who served as Assistant Pastor, and Reverend Miller Johnson are recognized for their love for the church and the ministerial services they provided.

 In January 1975, a unanimous call was extended to Reverend Cardes H. Brown, Jr., who accepted the call and officially assumed the duties of the pastor of New Light Missionary Baptist Church. The zealous leadership and visionary imminence of Pastor Brown are cited for tripling the membership at New Light. Many new ministries and community outreach programs were implemented under his guidance, which included plans to continue the drive for a new church facility.

            On May 9, 1976, the groundbreaking ceremony was held for our current worship center. Then, as a result of yet another pastoral vision, on December 3, 2000 the groundbreaking ceremony for the C.H. Brown Jr. Family Life Center was held. This new addition to the New Light campus was erected to meet the needs of its growing congregation and the community. On December 1, 2002, the Ribbon Cutting Service was held. Today, New Light Missionary Baptist Church and Family Life Center, is a beautifully edifice located on hallowed grounds at 1105 Willow Road. It stands with open doors to all. We humbly and reverently bow in submission with Praise and Thanksgiving to our Heavenly Father who has guided our every step, with His loving care.

sections.text:
Home

Contact us	



		
Home
About Us
Our Leadership
Media
Contact Us
Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala
Our Pulpit

There are a variety of services for worshippers of all ages at New Light Missionary Baptist Church. All week long, come, meet God, give thanks and nourish your spirit.

Sunday Worship Services
Location: Sanctuary
Sunday, 7:30am & 10:45am
Nursery available for children ages 18 months-4 years old

Children’s Church
Location: Children's Church Building
First, second and fourth Sunday each month
Sunday, 11:00 a.m.

Children’s Church is a safe, welcoming place where preschoolers learn about the love of God and the Word of God. This is specifically for elementary school children. Caring leaders share their love for Jesus and children, using bible stories, activities and games to introduce children to God’s wonder and His promises. Nurturing these young hearts, leaders help children learn that God loves them always.

MidWeek Manna (Worship on Wednesday) 
Location: Sanctuary
Every Wednesday, 12pm & 7pm 

MidWeek Manna is a great worship opportunity for those who missed the Sunday services and also for those who want a deeper relationship with God during the week. Our 12pm service is roughly 20-30 minutes long with lunch provided at the end. Transportation is provided. 

Sunday School 

Location: Various Classrooms

Every Sunday: 9:30am 

Bible Study

Adults/Location: Sanctuary/ Every Monday, 6:30-8:00pm

Singles/ Location: C.H. Brown Family Life Center/ Every Tuesday, 7:00pm

Seniors/Adults- Location: Lower Level Conference Room/ Every Thursday, 11:30am-12:30pm

NLMBC Video Release Policy: By entering this Church, you hereby grant the New Light Baptist Church the right, but not the obligation, to photograph, film and capture you and your likeness, on film and/or through any other method currently available or that becomes available in the future. You further agree that these images may be used in the production, exhibition, distribution, promotion, publicity and advertising of similar events and any other event sponsored by the New Light Missionary Baptist Church. Under no circumstances will your likeness be used in connection with commercial endorsements of any kind.

mission.title: New Light Missionary Baptist Church - Mission

sections.text:
Home	


Email Login	


Contact us	



		
Home
About Us
Our Leadership
Media
Contact Us
Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala

  MISSION         

The Mission of New Light Missionary Baptist Church is to glorify God, to know Christ, and to make Christ known through nurturing the body, ministering to the needs of its members, and evangelizing the lost through local and global missions. WE ARE ONE!

Our Position
Inspiration and inerrancy of the WORD of God.  The Bible is our sole authority for faith and practice. Creation of man by the direct act of God, and that man fell through sin, becoming spiritually dead. Incarnation, virgin birth, and deity of our Lord Jesus Christ. Vicarious atonement for the sins of Mankind by the shedding of the blood of Jesus on the cross. Bodily resurrection, glorious ascension and imminent personal return of Jesus Christ. Necessity of salvation by grace through faith in Christ alone. Priesthood of every believer. Local church as the divine institution for the spreading of the Gospel and defense of the faith. 

 

Our Proclamation
CHRIST DIED FOR YOUR SINS.  THE BIBLE SAYS...

Repent of your sin.  "For all have sinned." Romans 3: 23.  "Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish." Luke 13: 3

Believe the Gospel.  "He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on Him." John 3: 36

Receive the Lord Jesus Christ as your Savior.  "But as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name." John 1:12

Repent of your sin.  "For all have sinned." Romans 3: 23.  "Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish." Luke 13: 3

Believe the Gospel.  "He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on Him." John 3: 36

Receive the Lord Jesus Christ as your Savior.  "But as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name." John 1:12

MISSION         

The Mission of New Light Missionary Baptist Church is to glorify God, to know Christ, and to make Christ known through nurturing the body, ministering to the needs of its members, and evangelizing the lost through local and global missions. WE ARE ONE!

Our Position
Inspiration and inerrancy of the WORD of God.  The Bible is our sole authority for faith and practice. Creation of man by the direct act of God, and that man fell through sin, becoming spiritually dead. Incarnation, virgin birth, and deity of our Lord Jesus Christ. Vicarious atonement for the sins of Mankind by the shedding of the blood of Jesus on the cross. Bodily resurrection, glorious ascension and imminent personal return of Jesus Christ. Necessity of salvation by grace through faith in Christ alone. Priesthood of every believer. Local church as the divine institution for the spreading of the Gospel and defense of the faith. 

 

Our Proclamation
CHRIST DIED FOR YOUR SINS.  THE BIBLE SAYS...

Repent of your sin.  "For all have sinned." Romans 3: 23.  "Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish." Luke 13: 3

Believe the Gospel.  "He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on Him." John 3: 36

Receive the Lord Jesus Christ as your Savior.  "But as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name." John 1:12

The Mission of New Light Missionary Baptist Church is to glorify God, to know Christ, and to make Christ known through nurturing the body, ministering to the needs of its members, and evangelizing the lost through local and global missions. WE ARE ONE!

Our Position
Inspiration and inerrancy of the WORD of God.  The Bible is our sole authority for faith and practice. Creation of man by the direct act of God, and that man fell through sin, becoming spiritually dead. Incarnation, virgin birth, and deity of our Lord Jesus Christ. Vicarious atonement for the sins of Mankind by the shedding of the blood of Jesus on the cross. Bodily resurrection, glorious ascension and imminent personal return of Jesus Christ. Necessity of salvation by grace through faith in Christ alone. Priesthood of every believer. Local church as the divine institution for the spreading of the Gospel and defense of the faith.

Our Proclamation
CHRIST DIED FOR YOUR SINS.  THE BIBLE SAYS...
Repent of your sin.  "For all have sinned." Romans 3: 23.  "Except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish." Luke 13: 3
Believe the Gospel.  "He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on Him." John 3: 36
Receive the Lord Jesus Christ as your Savior.  "But as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name." John 1:12

pastor.title: New Light Missionary Baptist Church - Our Pastor

sections.text:
Home	


Email Login	


Contact us	



		
Home
About Us
Our Leadership
Media
Contact Us
Giving
Summer Adventure
50th Anniversary Gala
ABOUT PASTOR BROWN


Reverend Dr. Cardes H. Brown, Jr., is a true visionary and leader. As the son of the late Reverend Cardes H. Brown, Sr., and the late Mrs. Jocile Powell Brown, Dr. Brown was born in Rocky Mount, North Carolina, and attended Booker T. Washington High School. At nineteen years of age, during his sophomore year at North Carolina A&T State University, he accepted the calling of God to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ.

For over sixty years, Pastor Brown has devoted himself to pastoral ministry. Over the course of his ministerial career, he has been privileged to serve as pastor of various churches across North Carolina. For fifty years, he has served as the Senior Pastor of the New Light Missionary Baptist Church in Greensboro, North Carolina.

Dr. Brown received his Graduate of Theology degree and Bachelor of Arts degree from the Greensboro Bible College, a Master of Divinity degree from Shaw Divinity School in Raleigh, North Carolina, a Doctor of Ministry / Worship Studies from Eastern Carolina Christian College in Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina, an Honorary Doctor of Humane Letters from Apex School of Theology in Durham, North Carolina, and an Honorary Doctor of Divinity degree from the South Eastern University in Greenville, South Carolina. He is also a member of the distinguished Morehouse College Martin Luther King, Jr. Board of Preachers and is a proud member of the Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated. 

Dr. Brown is a man of many hats, with a heart for social justice and a deep commitment to his community. Having served on numerous boards and with several organizations, such as the state and local NAACP, Pulpit Forum of Greensboro, and Maya Angelou Center for Health Equity. In 2019, as a testament to his unwavering commitment to justice, equity, and equality, Dr. Brown was the recipient of the Benjamin L. Hooks Keeper of the Flame Award along with the Order of the Long Leaf Pine, which is one of the highest awards given to persons in North Carolina, who have made significant contributions to the state and their communities through their exemplary service and exceptional accomplishments. He is the Founder of  the Justice Coalition USA, which is an organization that seeks to bring political, educational, social, and economic justice to those members of humanity who suffer injustices in various organizations and governments.

Alongside him in ministry and life is the lovely Diane Pierce Brown. They are blessed with four children, six grandchildren, and two great-grandchildren. Dr. Brown is a warm and compassionate individual with a heart full of love and a spirit full of grace. He is undoubtedly inspiring to all who know him and his diligent work for Christ, the church, and community.
